comment: My opinion is that this plugin is simply fantastic, and I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone who wants code generated on their behalf. It works quickly, is intuitive, and significantly speeds up a developer’s work. The only drawback is the credit limit for users of the free version and the need to purchase a subscription to use it without restrictions. I really wish the plugin had no limits and didn’t reset every 16 days. I appeal to the authors to remove these restrictions so we can use the plugin’s capabilities without interruptions or limitations.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Latest nightly release has broken plan/agent mode. When in plan mode it continues implementing code without waiting for approval and proper steps.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Absolutely useless, I'm using remote client from windows to modify files from project on linux. It never worked, currently the AI writes duplicated files on Windows, even though codebase is not there.... It has been broken for almost a year.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: I don't understand other comments. This plugin works very well based on my experience. I use it every day and I don't have any major issues. Sometimes I get errors ofc but mostly I know what I'm doing wrong that time so it's easy to change the way.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Amazing tool! It really speed up my programming. I also really appreciate frequent updates and new features team are delivering for the plugin.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: It is better to not publish this plugin. Awful UX, hanging all the time, disconnecting, constant errors that are not clear what are about.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Does not give autocompletions when you want anymore. Only works maybe 1/10 times when you type. github-copilot-intellij-1.5.66-243 is a known working version, going to downgrade yet again as this is still not fixed.

comment: should be much easier to install and login with corporate account

comment:
Ok if you have more than 10GiB memory to spare. Last week my copilot-language-server process grew even larger than 20GiB.

I'm actually surprised this plugin still scores 2.3 stars, as it's pretty unusable.

comment: Copilot CLI works with MCP integrations, but I just spent two days trying to make the plugin version work with MCP, and nope!

The implementation feels broken, and the context management is highly unreliable—it constantly "forgets" what I just told it or drops project context mid-task.

comment: I am extremely disappointed. I’ve only been using the service for 3 hours today, consuming less than 4% of my monthly quota, yet I’m being rate-limited for 205,843 seconds (over 57 hours). This is unacceptable for a paid subscription.

comment: The chat pane now opens automatically whenever I launch IntelliJ IDEA. This is awful - I should be in charge of when and how I use Copilot, not Microsoft. So close to uninstalling this purely for this intrusive behaviour.

comment: this plugin is genuinely horrible and i'd give it -5 stars if i could, breaks all the time, now it leaves zombie processes on macOS. its only saving grace is the fact that Supermaven got killed off and OpenAI / Anthropic haven't developed a replacement for it yet. do the world a favor and delete this extension

comment:
It's not that bad, I use it daily and mostly happy about IDE integration. I would love to see:

  • Message with interruption (like in Zed)

  • Less bugs :)

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: I don’t usually write reviews, but after using GitHub Copilot for 2 years, I felt I should share this. The recent version has been frustrating — it’s buggy and often hits rate limit errors that break my workflow. It also feels like some useful features and models, like Claude, that I relied on are no longer available. Because of this, I’m considering trying other tools and hope these issues get fixed soon.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
All I want is the autocomplete from yester-year, without 1GB-7GB+++ copilot-language-server garbage and other other "Agent" features.

latest bug is that copilot-language-server isn't killed when the IDE is closed.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: It's amazing how Microslopt, instead of producing tools, produce internal power point presentations. I have no other explanation for poor implementation (memory hog) and deprecating the only useful mode "Edit".

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: No, opus in pro+, just the insanely overpriced 4.7? bye.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Things became better since 2024, but now a different problem raises: Copilot loses to other competitors. The only modern LLM it has to offer is Haiku 4.5, while others available are obsoleted by now. Why should I give it my API keys to use up-to-date LLMs in there when I can use official integrations, like Codex or Claude Code? It doesn't seem like Copilot is doing super-magic under the hood that out-weights competitors. Their coding assistants are good too!

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: 1.8.0-243 version is crap. copilot-language-server process is consuming about 24Gb of RAM and all available CPU resources. I had to manually downgrade it to the previous version.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Guys, I was one of the first users who adopted this plugin in 2022 and I love it. Unfortunately, I have to admit that the latest releases have had major performance issues, so much so that I've had to deactivate the plugin. The IDE is completely frozen and unusable. Please, I implore you to test the plugin better before releasing it. It's impossible to work like this; it's unusable. I have an enterprise account...

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Slows down the IDE drastically

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Ok, here we go. I normally do not write reviews. As a pycharm lover, I of course used the plugin and was wondering why the reviews are soooo bad. And then it started... Weird stuff was happening.

  • freezes
  • no good memory
  • slow ide
  • bullshit code generation for larger projects
  • requests just failed
  • and much more

Just, and I am sorry about this, in every way bad. And then because of some comments here, I switched to VScode. And WOW, this is soooooo much better. I had no idea how much fun this is. Its night and day. Smooth, no bugs, good code.

I am sorry about this jetbrains. You made one of the best IDEs and in the times of AI coding you messed up big time. You will probably loose most of your customers if you do not MASSIVELY overhaul this.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Slowing down IDE leading to crashes after some time. Also for whatever reason, copy & paste from chat window is broken. No history (at least I could not find it) for inline-chats.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Idk what is wrong with this plugin because it keeps crashing requests on "Oops, you reached the rate limit. Please try again later. " or just random errors. This breaks the workflow completely and just eats premium tokens without completing tasks. Really pisses me off to use it. Idk what rate limit this stupid thing is talking about... Copilot CLI never does that. So don't bother with this idiotic plugin just use CLI and be happy programmer.

comment: Extremely unreliable, especially when using it inline.

comment: Unable to adjust the height of the dialog box.

comment: terrible product

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: It's ok but still lacks lots of features.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Something keeps indexing in the background and consuming an insane amount of RAM and CPU while also taking up disk space. Waiting for it to finish does not help, because it just starts again after certain actions. It also clearly ignores excluded files, which is a major problem in a monorepo.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Bad plugin.

  • Sometimes the edit is made by the model but no changes are reflected to the file.
  • No thinking level for models.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Worst plugin, always lost connection or stuck on "Working..." but spend my tokens all the time and there is no conpensation. Use Claude directly instead.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: No support for OpenAI-compatible BYOK, like Ollama. In the "Manage Models" window, there's only OpenAI, which asks for an OpenAI key. Even if I add my OpenAI key, there's no way to add a custom endpoint to point to my Ollama or any other provider for BYOK.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Lately, this has been one of the most frequently used IDE plugins. It helps me with literally everything – managing agents, adding lines, naming commits, and conducting reviews. It's simply superb!

One inconvenience: agents constantly forget they can read and write files and try to output something to themselves via heredoc, which just freezes in the pyCharm console. I have to pause the conversation and explain, "Don't use heredoc, you can read and write directly."

Sometimes I want to have multiple conversations running in parallel, rather than just one in focus. For example, one is preparing a review, while the other is changing something. Currently, only one is available. I have to maintain a second copy of the project for parallelization.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer
